reasoning: Peer set (Denzi helmet sprites) shows 6 critical style traits the candidate lacks. (2) DETAIL DENSITY: Peers are packed with sub-pixel shading, multi-tone fills, and fine pixel work reflecting polished Denzi craft. Candidate is a blocky ~8x8 chunky flower with 3-4 flat color regions — far lower density. (3) SHADOW DIRECTION/COUNT: Peers have clear directional shadow volumes (lower-right or consistent across all). Candidate has no discernible shadow pass. (4) HIGHLIGHT INTENSITY: Peers have specular or ambient occlusion-style highlights on surfaces. Candidate has none visible. (5) RENDERING STYLE: Peers read as isometric-adjacent RPG item icons with volumetric pixel shading. Candidate reads as a flat 2D icon, different paradigm entirely. (6) CHARM LEVEL: Peers are polished, tight, cute Denzi sprites. Candidate looks unfinished/placeholder — the pixel budget is too coarse and the forms too simple to sit beside the peer set. Color temperature and palette differences are expected (helmets vs flora) and NOT penalized per instructions, but the six structural rendering failures above make this a clear fail at score 3.
